lede: A look at May 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Zach Edey and Frank Gore, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2022 Buffalo shooting
events.desc: Ten people are killed in a mass shooting in Buffalo, New York.

events.title: Salvadoran Civil War
events.desc: Salvadoran Civil War: The Sumpul River massacre occurs in Chalatenango, El Salvador.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: Eight Communist bloc countries, including the Soviet Union, sign a mutual defense treaty called the Warsaw Pact.

events.title: Talyllyn Railway
events.desc: Trains run on the Talyllyn Railway in Wales for the first time since preservation, making it the first railway in the world to be operated by volunteers.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Rotterdam, Netherlands, is bombed by the Luftwaffe of Nazi Germany despite a ceasefire, killing about 900 people and destroying the historic city center.

events.title: Harry Hands
events.desc: Cape Town Mayor, Sir Harry Hands, inaugurates the two-minute silence.

events.title: Salem witchcraft trial (1878)
events.desc: The last witchcraft trial held in the United States begins in Salem, Massachusetts, after Lucretia Brown, an adherent of Christian Science, accused Daniel Spofford of attempting to harm her through his mental powers.

events.title: Boshin War
events.desc: Boshin War: The Battle of Utsunomiya Castle ends as former Tokugawa shogunate forces withdraw northward.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: During the Vicksburg campaign, Union forces drive Confederates under Joseph E. Johnston out of Jackson, Mississippi in the Battle of Jackson.

events.title: Mindon Min
events.desc: Mindon Min is crowned as King of Burma in Mandalay, Burma.

births.name: Sabine Schmitz
births.line: German race car driver and sportscaster (died 2021)

births.name: Rudy Pérez
births.line: Cuban-born American composer and music producer

births.name: Alistair McAlpine, Baron McAlpine of West Green
Charles Beyer
births.line: German-English engineer, co-founded Beyer, Peacock & Company (died 1876)

deaths.text: Hans van Houwelingen, Dutch mathematician and professor emeritus of medical statistics
Asghar Ali Engineer, Indian author and activist
Keizō Obuchi, Japanese politician, 84th Prime Minister of Japan

observances.text: Christian feast day: Justa, Justina and Henedina
Christian feast day: Matthias the Apostle (Roman Catholic Church, Anglican Communion, Evangelical Lutheran Church in America)
Christian feast day: Mo Chutu of Lismore (Roman Catholic Church)
